title: Dye
description: Library to expose INVOKEDYNAMIC to mods.
body:
A library / modlauncher service that exposes the jvm INVOKEDYNAMIC opcode to modders. For more information on how to use it as a modder, see the [GitHub](https://github.com/noeppi-noeppi/Dye).

Some more explanation on this:

The Java Virtual Machine (JVM) has an opcode called [INVOKEDYNAMIC](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/specs/jvms/se16/html/jvms-6.html#jvms-6.5.invokedynamic). Each individual occurrence of that opcode in bytecode is permanently bound to a [CallSite](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/16/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/invoke/CallSite.html) object when executed the first time. This CallSite is obtained by calling a bootstrap method, which is implemented in plain java. This is for example used to implement lambdas in bytecode.

Dye provides a transform service to replace regular method calls to methods annotated with a special annotation with INVOKEDYNAMIC opcodes to a custom bootstrap method given by the annotation value.
